list.help: List Venmo transactions visible to the authenticated account.

options.name: --before-id
options.help: Return transactions older than this story id (pagination)

list.usage_instructions: List recent Venmo transactions. Default limit 50, JSON output. By design, each record is the FULL raw Venmo API payload (top-level keys include `id`, `type`, `note`, `date_created`, `date_updated`, `audience`, `transaction_external_id`, `payment` (nested object with `id`/`status`/`action`/`amount`/`audience`/`note`/`actor`/`target`/`date_completed`/`date_authorized`/`date_reminded`/`external_wallet_payment_info`/`participants`/`merchant_split_purchase`), `app`, `likes`, `reactions`, `comments`, `mentions`, `transfer`, `authorization`) plus a CLI-injected `payment_id` top-level convenience field. `--filter` and `--properties` BOTH support dotted paths into the nested record (e.g. `--filter payment.amount:gt:100`, `--filter payment.status:eq:settled`, `--filter payment.action:eq:pay`, `--filter note:contains:lego`, `--properties payment_id,payment.amount,payment.actor.display_name,payment.target.user.display_name,note`). `--before-id <payment_id>` paginates to older transactions. `--profile <name>` queries a specific Venmo auth profile without changing the active profile. `--table` renders a curated subset of columns; pass `--properties` to override the column set. The output envelope is `{"cache_hit": bool, "results": [...]}` — iterate via `jq '.results[]'`.

get.help: Look up a single transaction by id from recent history.
get.usage_instructions: Look up a single transaction by `payment_id` (the CLI-injected top-level field; also at `payment.id`). NOTE: top-level `id` in the raw payload is Venmo's `story_id` which is null for items returned by /stories/target-or-actor — always use `payment_id` here. Searches the most recent 500 transactions. Returns the same full raw record shape as one entry from `transactions list`. `--profile <name>` queries a specific Venmo auth profile without changing the active profile. `--table` and `--properties` (dotted paths) behave the same as on `list`. Example: `venmo transactions get 4418053612741823878 --profile personal --table`.

transactions.usage_instructions: Read-only transaction history. Each record is the FULL raw Venmo API payload (the unmodified `Transaction._json` from venmo-api) plus a CLI-injected top-level `payment_id` for convenience. The shape is nested — scalar fields live both at the top level (`id`/story_id, `type`, `note`, `date_created`, `date_updated`, `audience`, `transaction_external_id`, `payment_id`) and inside the nested `payment` object (`payment.id`, `payment.status`, `payment.action`, `payment.amount`, `payment.actor.*` user object, `payment.target.user.*` user object, `payment.target.merchant`, `payment.date_completed`). Related collections include `likes`, `reactions`, `comments`, `mentions`, `app`, `transfer`, `authorization`. Both `--filter` and `--properties` accept dotted paths (`payment.amount:gt:100`, `payment.actor.username:eq:example-user`).

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Authenticate with Venmo. Reads `venmo-username` and `venmo-password` from the CLI-tools keychain (`<repo-root>/secret-manager/secrets.sh`). Triggers an SMS OTP from Venmo; prompt for the 6-digit code interactively, or set `VENMO_OTP=<code>` env var. Use `--force` to clear saved tokens and re-authenticate. Example: `VENMO_OTP=123456 venmo auth login --force`.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored access token and device id for the active profile. Does not remove the keychain-stored username/password.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Show authentication status across all profiles. Performs a LIVE round-trip via the saved access token; reports `authenticated: true` only if the token still works.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Not applicable for Venmo — Venmo access tokens are long-lived and do not expire unless explicitly revoked. This command exists only because it is part of the shared auth scaffold.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run a live API round-trip (fetch the authenticated user's profile) and report whether the stored access token still works. Use this after a long gap to confirm the session is alive.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all configured profiles. Shows which one is the default and whether each is authenticated.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show the credentials and metadata for a named profile.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile. After creation, run `venmo auth login --profile <name>` to authenticate it.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Switch the default profile that commands use when no `--profile` flag is passed.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and all its stored credentials.

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage named authentication profiles. Each profile has its own access token, device id, and cache directory. Useful for switching between multiple Venmo accounts.

auth.usage_instructions: Manage Venmo authentication. The CLI does NOT prompt for username/password — both are read from the CLI-tools keychain (`venmo-username`, `venmo-password`). On first login, Venmo sends an SMS OTP; pass it via the `VENMO_OTP` env var for non-interactive use, or type it at the interactive prompt. After login, an access token and device id are saved to the per-profile .env; future logins skip OTP because the device is trusted.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Delete all cached venmo responses for the active profile. Run this when you want to force a fresh API call.

cache.usage_instructions: Inspect and clear the response cache used by the shared `@cached` decorator. Cache files live under `~/.local/share/cli-tools/venmo/.profiles/<profile>/cache/`.

usage_instructions: Use the `venmo` CLI to retrieve Venmo transaction history. Venmo has no public API; this tool wraps the reverse-engineered `venmo-api` Python library (private mobile API at api.venmo.com). Commands follow `venmo <group> <action>` pattern. The CLI is read-only by design — only `transactions list` and `transactions get` are exposed. Authentication is username+password+SMS-OTP; credentials are pulled from the CLI-tools keychain (`venmo-username`, `venmo-password`). After `venmo auth login`, a long-lived access token is saved and re-used. RECORD SHAPE: transaction records are the FULL raw Venmo API payload (unmodified `Transaction._json`) plus a CLI-injected top-level `payment_id` convenience field. Top-level keys are nested objects; use dotted paths for both `--filter` and `--properties` (e.g. `--filter payment.amount:gt:100`, `--properties payment_id,payment.amount,payment.actor.display_name,note`). Default output is JSON wrapped in {"cache_hit": bool, "results": [...]} for list commands. Use `--table` for human-readable output (curated columns: payment_id, date_created, type, payment.action, payment.amount, payment.status, payment.actor.display_name, payment.target.user.display_name, note). Pagination via `--before-id`.
